project.name: DegreeForge
project.description: Interactive 4-year degree planner + next-semester schedule optimizer for UT Austin ECE

architecture_decisions.proxy: Express -- single /api/chat endpoint for Claude API key isolation
architecture_decisions.structure: Monorepo with npm workspaces -- packages/client/ + packages/server/
architecture_decisions.styling: shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS -- professional accessible components
architecture_decisions.persistence: localStorage + JSON export/import for plan state
architecture_decisions.solver: Deterministic code (toposort + constraint satisfaction) -- not LLM
architecture_decisions.normalization: E E -> ECE at data load time

recent_decisions: Vite + React + TypeScript (no Next.js -- overkill for localhost SPA)
Express proxy for Claude API (not direct frontend calls)
shadcn/ui + Tailwind (professional components for progress bars, tooltips, dialogs)
V1 + V2 ship together (schedule optimizer in scope)
Normalize E E -> ECE at data load time
Grade distributions as primary quality signal (not RMP)

TASK-001.title: Project scaffold (monorepo, Vite, Express, Tailwind, shadcn)
TASK-001.description: Scaffold monorepo with npm workspaces. packages/client/ = Vite + React + TS + Tailwind + shadcn/ui. packages/server/ = Express + TypeScript. Root package.json with workspace scripts. Copy data/ JSONs into client public folder. Confirm npm run dev starts both.
acceptance_criteria: npm install from root installs both workspaces
npm run dev starts Vite dev server + Express proxy concurrently
Tailwind + shadcn/ui renders a test component
Express /api/health returns 200
data/ JSON files accessible from client
TASK-001.why: Foundation -- nothing else builds without this

TASK-002.title: Data layer + TypeScript types + E E->ECE normalization
TASK-002.description: Define TypeScript interfaces for all 9 JSON schemas. Build data loader hooks (useCourseCatalog, usePrereqGraph, etc.). Implement E E -> ECE normalization at load time. Export a unified DataContext provider.
acceptance_criteria: TypeScript interfaces for all 9 data files
React context provider with all data loaded and typed
All E E prefixes normalized to ECE in grade distribution data
Custom hooks for each data domain
Unit tests for normalization logic
TASK-002.why: All features consume typed data; normalization must happen once at load

TASK-003.title: Prerequisite graph engine (DAG, toposort, validation)
TASK-003.description: Build prerequisite graph as a DAG class. Methods: getPrereqs(course), getCoreqs(course), getDownstream(course), topologicalSort(courses), validatePlacement(course, semester, plan). All pure TypeScript, no React.
acceptance_criteria: DAG class with adjacency list representation
Topological sort returns valid course ordering
validatePlacement returns specific violation details
getDownstream returns all transitively dependent courses
Handles corequisites (same-semester-or-earlier)
Unit tests for all graph operations
TASK-003.why: Core logic the solver, validation UI, and what-if all depend on

TASK-004.title: Constraint solver / recommendation engine
TASK-004.description: Build the Recommend 4-Year Plan engine. Takes remaining requirements, prereq graph, tech core, user profile (load tolerance), pinned courses. Distributes courses across semesters respecting prereqs, coreqs, offering patterns, course load limits, and pins.
acceptance_criteria: Generates valid semester-by-semester plan for Adi's profile
Respects prerequisite ordering (no prereq violations in output)
Respects offering patterns (fall-only, spring-only)
Honors pinned courses as fixed constraints
Configurable max courses per semester
Returns structured plan data (not UI)
Unit tests with known-good plans
TASK-004.why: The Recommend 4-Year Plan button -- needs graph engine first

TASK-005.title: App shell + page layout + routing
TASK-005.description: Create app shell with two pages: V1 Planner and V2 Scheduler. Use React Router (client-side). Build header with nav, main layout containers for timeline grid + sidebar. Dark/light mode toggle.
acceptance_criteria: Two routes: / (planner) and /schedule (scheduler)
Header with nav links between V1 and V2
Main layout: left area for timeline, right area for palette/sidebar
Responsive enough for desktop (no mobile needed)
DataContext provider wraps entire app
TASK-005.why: V1 planner page + V2 scheduler page structure

TASK-006.title: Semester timeline grid + course cards
TASK-006.description: Build the horizontal semester timeline grid. Columns = semesters (past, current, future). Course cards show course number, title, category color, avg GPA badge. Past semesters are muted/checkmarked. Current semester highlighted. Future semesters have empty slots.
acceptance_criteria: Semester columns render from user profile data
Course cards show number, title, category color, GPA
Category colors: Blue=ECE core, Yellow=Gen ed, Green=Tech core, Gray=Free elective, Purple=Math
Past semesters visually distinct (muted)
Current semester highlighted
Empty slots in future semesters accept drops
TASK-006.why: Primary UI surface -- cards show course data, colors, GPA

TASK-007.title: Course palette panel
TASK-007.description: Build the right-side course palette with collapsible sections: Remaining ECE core, Tech core courses, Gen ed, Free electives, Math courses. Each card shows course number, title, avg GPA. Courses with unmet prereqs are dimmed.
acceptance_criteria: Collapsible/tabbed sections for each category
Cards match timeline card style
Courses with unmet prereqs visually dimmed
Remaining courses computed from profile + plan state
Search/filter within palette
TASK-007.why: Right-side panel with categorized remaining courses

TASK-008.title: Drag-drop system (dnd-kit: palette<->timeline)
TASK-008.description: Implement dnd-kit drag-and-drop: palette -> semester slot, semester -> semester, semester -> palette (remove). DndContext wraps timeline + palette. Sortable within semesters. Drop zones in empty slots.
acceptance_criteria: Drag from palette to semester slot
Drag between semesters
Drag back to palette (remove from plan)
Visual drag overlay with course card preview
Drop zone highlights on hover
Duplicate detection (can't place same course twice)
TASK-008.why: Connects palette and grid -- the core interaction model

TASK-009.title: Progress bars (credit hours, ECE, gen ed, tech core, electives)
TASK-009.description: Build progress bar components at top of planner page. Track: total credit hours (X/128), ECE core (X/Y), Core curriculum gen ed (X/8), Tech core (X/8), Free electives (X/11 hours). Update live on every plan change.
acceptance_criteria: Five progress bars with labels and counts
Bars update reactively on plan state changes
XP-bar visual style (filled portion with text)
Correct counting logic for each requirement category
Handles completed courses from profile
TASK-009.why: Real-time feedback on every plan change

TASK-010.title: Prerequisite validation UI (borders, tooltips, highlights)
TASK-010.description: On every course placement: red border if prereqs missing, orange border if coreqs misplaced, tooltip listing violations. On hover: highlight downstream dependent courses. On move: check if it invalidates dependents.
acceptance_criteria: Red border + tooltip for prereq violations
Orange border + tooltip for coreq violations
Hover highlights downstream courses with visual edges
Moving a course checks downstream impact
Validation runs on every plan change (drop, remove, reorder)
TASK-010.why: Red/orange borders + downstream impact on hover

TASK-011.title: What-if simulator (tech core switch, Math BA toggle, diffs)
TASK-011.description: UI for switching tech core tracks (dropdown of 9 tracks) and toggling Math BA double major. On change: recompute requirements, show diff summary (courses added/removed, credit hour impact, graduation timeline change), update progress bars, update palette.
acceptance_criteria: Tech core dropdown with all 9 tracks
Math BA toggle switch
Diff summary panel showing added/removed courses
Progress bars update on what-if change
Palette updates to show new required courses
Can revert what-if change
TASK-011.why: Needs solver for recompute + progress bars for updated tracking

TASK-012.title: Claude chat panel + Express proxy endpoint
TASK-012.description: Build collapsible chat panel (slide-out). Express POST /api/chat endpoint proxies to Claude API with key from .env. System prompt includes full plan state + prereq graph + user profile + grade data for relevant courses. Streaming response.
acceptance_criteria: Collapsible chat panel UI (slide-out from right or bottom)
Express /api/chat proxies to Claude with server-side API key
System prompt includes current plan state context
Streaming response renders token-by-token
Chat history persists within session
Error handling for API failures
TASK-012.why: Chat UI + /api/chat proxy -- needs plan state for system prompt

TASK-013.title: External links + course detail popover
TASK-013.description: On course card click/hover: popover with full prereqs, downstream courses, grade distribution summary chart, and external links (RMP, UTGradesPlus, Past Syllabi, CIS Surveys). Links open in new tab.
acceptance_criteria: Popover on course card click
Shows prerequisites and downstream courses
Grade distribution mini-chart (bar chart or similar)
RMP link with professor name pre-filled
UTGradesPlus / official grade distribution link
Past syllabi and CIS survey links
TASK-013.why: RMP, grade dist, syllabi links on course cards

TASK-014.title: State persistence (localStorage + JSON export/import)
TASK-014.description: Auto-save full plan state to localStorage on every change. Add Export Plan (downloads JSON) and Import Plan (file upload) buttons. On app load, restore from localStorage if available.
acceptance_criteria: Plan state auto-saves to localStorage on every change
App restores plan state from localStorage on load
Export button downloads plan as .json file
Import button accepts .json file and restores plan
Clear/reset plan button
Handles corrupted/invalid localStorage gracefully

TASK-015.title: V2: Schedule optimizer engine
TASK-015.description: Build the schedule optimizer: given a list of courses for next semester and fall-2026-sections.json, generate all conflict-free section combinations. Rank by weighted factors: avg GPA (highest), time preference, schedule fit, instruction mode. Return top 5 candidates.
acceptance_criteria: Generates all valid (no time conflict) section combinations
Weighted ranking: GPA > time pref > fit > mode
Returns top 5 ranked schedules
Each schedule includes per-section details (prof, time, room, GPA)
Composite score per schedule
Handles courses with multiple meeting times (lecture + discussion)
Unit tests with known section data
TASK-015.why: Generate conflict-free section combos, weighted ranking by GPA/time/fit

TASK-016.title: V2: Schedule calendar view + selection UI
TASK-016.description: Build V2 page: weekly calendar view (Mon-Fri grid, time on Y-axis). Show top 5 schedules as tabs/cards. Each shows all sections with color-coded course blocks. Click to select. Copy unique numbers button. Nudge to verify links (RMP, syllabi).
acceptance_criteria: Weekly calendar grid (Mon-Fri, 8am-9pm)
Color-coded course blocks with professor, time, room
Toggle between top 5 candidate schedules
Composite score + per-course GPA displayed
Copy unique numbers button for registration
Verify nudge links (RMP, CIS surveys, syllabi)
Input: select courses from V1 plan for next semester
TASK-016.why: Weekly calendar grid, schedule comparison, copy unique numbers

TASK-017.title: Integration testing + visual polish
TASK-017.description: End-to-end testing of full flow: load data, generate plan, drag courses, validate prereqs, switch tech core, chat with Claude, export/import, V2 schedule optimization. Visual consistency pass.
acceptance_criteria: Full user flow works end-to-end
No console errors in normal usage
Prereq validation catches all known edge cases
Chat panel context includes live plan state
V1 -> V2 flow (select next semester courses -> optimize schedule)
Export/import round-trips correctly
Visual consistency across all components
TASK-017.why: Connect all pieces, edge cases, visual consistency pass

decisions: Stack: Vite + React + TypeScript, Express proxy, Tailwind + shadcn/ui, dnd-kit
Monorepo with npm workspaces: packages/client/ + packages/server/
Express proxy for Claude API -- single /api/chat endpoint
shadcn/ui + Tailwind for professional accessible components
localStorage + JSON export/import for plan persistence
V1 + V2 ship together -- schedule optimizer is in scope
Normalize E E -> ECE at data load time
Constraint solver is deterministic code, not LLM
Grade distributions as primary quality signal (not RMP)
changelog: 2025-07-17: Built corpus agent with search, lookup, and 5 extractors
2025-07-17: Generated 5 core data files (course-catalog, prereq-graph, tech-cores, degree-reqs, offering-schedule)
2025-07-18: OCR'd 53 schedule page images, built math-requirements.json, fall-2026-sections.json, grade-distributions placeholder
2026-04-05: Parsed 5 grade distribution CSVs (249 courses), OCR'd ECE upper-div (62 courses, 232 sections), built user-profile.json
2026-04-05: Completed init-project Phases 5-9 -- plan approved, 17 tasks scaffolded, all architecture decisions locked
2026-04-05: auto-run configured -- 17 handoff files generated in .agents/handoffs/, auto_run block added to state.json

2026-04-06: TASK-001 complete -- monorepo scaffolded: Vite+React+TS+Tailwind+shadcn/ui (client), Express+TS (server), 9 JSON files copied to public/data/, tsc passes on both packages, both dev servers confirmed starting

2026-04-06: TASK-002 complete -- TypeScript interfaces for all 9 JSON schemas, normalizeEEtoECE() with 19 passing unit tests, DataProvider with 11 typed hooks, DataProvider wraps React tree in main.tsx, tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
